title: chenopodium album var. commune
intro: Chenopodium album var. commune, also known as the common lamb's quarters or fat hen, is a variety of the Chenopodium album plant, a type of annual herb belonging to the goosefoot family. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chenopodium album var. commune include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or made into a tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
